description_long: This house features a medieval Tudor-style design with a steeply pitched gable roof, exposed timber framing, and stone foundation. The exterior walls are a mix of timber and plaster, with prominent vertical and horizontal wooden beams. The roof is covered in weathered shingles, and a large stone chimney rises along one side. Windows are arranged in rows with wooden mullions, and a covered porch supported by stone columns extends along the front. A hanging sign suggests the building may serve a public or commercial function, such as an inn or tavern.
description: A medieval Tudor-style house with timber framing, stone foundation, steep gable roof, and a covered porch with a hanging sign.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch with stone columns, and a hanging sign to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep gable roof, stone chimney, and a projecting upper story with timber framing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the long row of windows, timber-framed upper story, and the full length of the covered porch.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a smaller gable projection and the continuation of the timber and plaster wall pattern.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a covered porch with stone columns, steps, and a hanging sign, with a wooden door set back under the overhang.
